meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Debug Faster: AI Strategies for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with basic app bugs. Master AI-driven debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code. Learn to ship professional SaaS faster than ever.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing syntax errors manually. By integrating Antigravity for automated logic analysis and Claude Code for CLI-based repairs, you reduce debugging time by 70%. This workflow allows you to diagnose complex state issues in seconds, ensuring your client projects remain profitable, high-quality, and delivered ahead of schedule every time.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, your client is waiting, and the terminal is bleeding red errors. We’ve all been there—staring at a basic function that refuses to execute, feeling the crushing weight of a deadline slipping away. It is the silent killer of the freelance career: getting stuck in the weeds of trivial bugs while your vision for the app remains unfinished.

But the era of manual debugging is dead. Professional developers now leverage AI agents to act as a tireless second pair of eyes, parsing logic flows and identifying bottlenecks before they crash your deployment. By mastering the synergy between Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform from a struggling coder into an elite architect capable of shipping production-ready SaaS at breakneck speeds.

steps.title: Establish the Contextual Baseline
steps.description: Before fixing, define the expected state versus the actual state to give the AI a clear success metric.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following function for state inconsistencies: [INSERT CODE]. Define the expected output vs the current unexpected behavior.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/target_file.ts --context 'Identify logic gaps in the function'

steps.title: Automated Logic Trace
steps.description: Use Antigravity to map the execution flow and identify where the data breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Trace the execution flow of this module. Highlight the specific line where variable state deviates from expected schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'trace execution of function X'

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Narrow the focus to prevent the AI from refactoring code that isn't broken.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal reproduction script for this bug. Isolate the logic to a single component.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/test_isolation.ts --instruction 'Extract the logic into a testable unit'

steps.title: Synthesize the Fix
steps.description: Generate the patch using Claude Code while maintaining your existing style guide.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a refactor for the identified bug. Ensure it follows functional programming patterns and handles null states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix --file ./src/target_file.ts --patch 'apply fix for variable state mismatch'

steps.title: Verify and Deploy
steps.description: Run automated checks to ensure the fix doesn't introduce regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the patch for potential side effects or performance regressions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm test && npm run build'

pro_tips: Always provide the AI with your project's 'style guide' or existing codebase patterns to ensure the generated code is indistinguishable from your own.
Use 'claude read' on your configuration files first; most bugs are environment-related, not logic-related.
When stuck, ask the AI to 'explain the bug like I am a senior engineer' to get deeper insights into architectural flaws rather than just syntax fixes.
Keep your Antigravity prompts atomic; one specific bug per prompt yields significantly higher accuracy than asking for a total refactor.
Leverage Claude Code's terminal history to identify recurring bug patterns across different freelance projects.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully patched your first bug, integrate these commands into a custom shell script to automate your diagnostic workflow for every new project.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the terminal and start building the future of your freelance business. With these tools in your kit, you can reclaim your time and scale your output effortlessly. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
